skill.name: shopify-apps
skill.description: Expert patterns for Shopify app development including Remix/React Router apps, embedded apps with App Bridge, webhook handling, GraphQL Admin API, Polaris components, billing, and app extensions. Use when: shopify app, shopify, embedded app, polaris, app bridge.
skill.summary: Guides Shopify app architecture, embedded app patterns, webhooks, billing, Polaris, and extensions.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged a high severity weak cryptography pattern at SKILL.md line 3. Manual review found only a reference to webhook HMAC verification in descriptive documentation, with no implementation, algorithm choice, secret handling, or prompt injection attempt present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: HMAC Mention In Description
low_findings.description: The static finding points to SKILL.md line 3, where the skill description mentions webhook handling. This is documentation about verifying Shopify webhooks, not code that selects or implements a weak cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file contains only instructional markdown and no executable cryptographic code. The flagged line does not name a weak algorithm or expose secret material.

content.user_title: Build Shopify Apps with Expert Patterns
content.value_statement: Shopify app teams need clear guidance across embedded apps, webhooks, billing, and Admin API design. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code focused patterns for modern Shopify app development.
actual_capabilities: Explains modern Shopify app setup with Remix or React Router patterns.
Guides embedded app design using Shopify App Bridge and Polaris.
Describes secure webhook handling with HMAC verification and asynchronous processing.
Recommends GraphQL Admin API patterns for new Shopify app code.
Highlights billing, app extension, rate limit, and GDPR handling considerations.
limitations: It provides guidance only and does not include runnable project files.
It does not connect to Shopify APIs or inspect live store data.
It does not replace Shopify documentation for current platform changes.
It does not implement authentication, billing, or webhook handlers by itself.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Embedded App
use_cases.description: Map the main architecture, admin embedding approach, webhook needs, and GraphQL Admin API usage before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Review Webhook and API Design
use_cases.description: Check whether webhook handling, rate limits, GDPR handlers, and Admin API patterns follow Shopify app expectations.

use_cases.title: Improve Admin User Interface
use_cases.description: Use Polaris and App Bridge guidance to create a consistent embedded app experience in Shopify Admin.

prompt_templates.title: Identify the Right Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: I am building a Shopify app for [business goal]. Recommend the main app patterns I should use and explain why.

prompt_templates.title: Design Embedded App Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an embedded Shopify app flow for [feature]. Include App Bridge, Polaris, navigation, and Admin API touchpoints.

prompt_templates.title: Review Webhook Handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Shopify webhook plan for security, response timing, retry behavior, HMAC verification, and asynchronous processing.

prompt_templates.title: Audit App Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Shopify app architecture for billing, GDPR handlers, GraphQL Admin API usage, rate limits, extensions, and deployment risks.

output_examples.input: Plan the core architecture for an embedded Shopify app that syncs product data.
output_examples.output: A structured architecture plan covering embedded admin flow, authentication, product sync jobs, webhook subscriptions, rate limit handling, and GraphQL Admin API boundaries.

output_examples.input: Review my webhook design for order updates.
output_examples.output: A review that checks immediate response behavior, HMAC verification, background processing, retries, idempotency, and GDPR handler coverage.

output_examples.input: Improve my Shopify admin extension user experience.
output_examples.output: A set of Polaris and App Bridge recommendations for navigation, loading states, form layout, feedback messages, and admin consistency.

best_practices: Use GraphQL Admin API for new Shopify app development unless a specific REST endpoint is required.
Verify webhook HMAC values and respond quickly before doing background work.
Plan billing, GDPR handlers, and protected customer data access before public launch.
anti_patterns: Building new app features around legacy REST API patterns without a clear reason.
Doing long webhook processing before sending the HTTP response.
Polling Shopify data when a reliable webhook can trigger the workflow.

faq.question: Does this skill generate a complete Shopify app?
faq.answer: No. It provides architecture and implementation guidance, not a complete runnable app template.

faq.question: Can it help with embedded Shopify apps?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers embedded app patterns with App Bridge, Polaris, and Shopify Admin workflows.

faq.question: Does it cover webhook security?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends HMAC verification and quick webhook responses with asynchronous processing.

faq.question: Does it support Shopify billing design?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide billing considerations and launch readiness, but it does not execute billing flows.

faq.question: Can it replace Shopify documentation?
faq.answer: No. Use it for focused guidance, then confirm current platform requirements in Shopify documentation.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
